# `unity mcp` command reference

Generated from Unity CLI `1.0.0-beta.2` help output.

Installed help is authoritative; this file is a versioned snapshot.

## unity mcp

- CLI version: `1.0.0-beta.2`
- Supported platforms: linux, macos, windows
- Aliases: None

MCP server and client configuration for Unity Editor

### Usage

`unity mcp [options] [command]`

### Subcommands

| Name | Aliases | Summary |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `configure` | — | Write MCP server entry for an AI agent client Run 'unity mcp configure --list' to see all clients |

### Positional arguments

None.

### Options

| Option | Value | Required | Repeatable | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project-path` | `<path>` | False | False | — | Locate the Editor by its project path; with configure, pin the entry to that project |
| `--runtime` | `<version>` | False | False | — | Filter by Unity runtime version |
| `--runtime-path` | `<path>` | False | False | — | Directory containing the Unity Player port descriptor file |
| `-h`, `--help` | — | False | False | — | display help for command |
| `-V`, `--version` | — | False | False | — | output the version number |
| `--format` | `<format>` | False | False | — | Output format: human, json, tsv, ndjson (env: UNITY_FORMAT) |
| `--no-banner` | — | False | False | — | Suppress the startup banner (env: UNITY_NO_BANNER) |
| `--non-interactive` | — | False | False | — | Disable interactive prompts. Useful in CI/CD environments. (env: UNITY_NON_INTERACTIVE) |
| `--quiet` | — | False | False | — | Suppress informational output (env: UNITY_QUIET) |
| `--verbose` | — | False | False | — | Show full error details including stack traces on failure (env: UNITY_VERBOSE) |
| `--proxy` | `<url>` | False | False | — | HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS/PAC proxy URL. Examples: http://user:pass@host:8080, socks5://host:1080, pac+http://wpad/proxy.pac (env: UNITY_PROXY) |
| `--proxy-disable` | — | False | False | — | Disable proxy for this invocation, ignoring all sources |
| `--log-proxy` | — | False | False | — | Log every outbound request to proxy-request.json for this run. Off by default; typically used once when reproducing a proxy issue for support. Also settable via UNITY_LOG_PROXY=1 or the proxyRequestLogging user setting. (env: UNITY_LOG_PROXY) |
| `--no-log-proxy` | — | False | False | — | Opt out of --log-proxy / UNITY_LOG_PROXY / the persisted user setting for this run. Use when logging is enabled globally but you want one clean invocation. |

### Environment variables

| Variable | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `UNITY_FORMAT` | Mirrors --format. |
| `UNITY_NO_BANNER` | Mirrors --no-banner. |
| `UNITY_NON_INTERACTIVE` | Mirrors --non-interactive. |
| `UNITY_QUIET` | Mirrors --quiet. |
| `UNITY_VERBOSE` | Mirrors --verbose. |
| `UNITY_PROXY` | Mirrors --proxy. |
| `UNITY_LOG_PROXY` | Mirrors --log-proxy. |

### Examples exposed by help

`unity mcp                                   Start the MCP stdio server`
`unity mcp --project-path /path/to/MyProject Start server for a specific project`
`unity mcp configure claude                  Configure Claude Desktop`
`unity mcp configure --list                  Show all supported clients`

### Exit codes

None.

Confirm behavior with `unity mcp --help` on the target machine before execution.

## unity mcp configure

- CLI version: `1.0.0-beta.2`
- Supported platforms: linux, macos, windows
- Aliases: None

Write MCP server config for an AI agent client

### Usage

`unity mcp configure [options] [client]`

### Subcommands

None.

### Positional arguments

| Argument | Required | Repeatable | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `client` | True | False | Client to configure (claude, cursor, vscode, windsurf, …) |

### Options

| Option | Value | Required | Repeatable | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--list` | — | False | False | — | List all supported clients and their config paths |
| `--local` | — | False | False | — | Write project-local config (for clients that support it, e.g. cursor, windsurf) |
| `--yes` | — | False | False | — | Skip the "already exists, update?" confirmation prompt |
| `--dry-run` | — | False | False | — | Print what would be written without modifying any files |
| `-h`, `--help` | — | False | False | — | display help for command |
| `--project-path` | `<path>` | False | False | — | Locate the Editor by its project path; with configure, pin the entry to that project |
| `--runtime` | `<version>` | False | False | — | Filter by Unity runtime version |
| `--runtime-path` | `<path>` | False | False | — | Directory containing the Unity Player port descriptor file |
| `-V`, `--version` | — | False | False | — | output the version number |
| `--format` | `<format>` | False | False | — | Output format: human, json, tsv, ndjson (env: UNITY_FORMAT) |
| `--no-banner` | — | False | False | — | Suppress the startup banner (env: UNITY_NO_BANNER) |
| `--non-interactive` | — | False | False | — | Disable interactive prompts. Useful in CI/CD environments. (env: UNITY_NON_INTERACTIVE) |
| `--quiet` | — | False | False | — | Suppress informational output (env: UNITY_QUIET) |
| `--verbose` | — | False | False | — | Show full error details including stack traces on failure (env: UNITY_VERBOSE) |
| `--proxy` | `<url>` | False | False | — | HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS/PAC proxy URL. Examples: http://user:pass@host:8080, socks5://host:1080, pac+http://wpad/proxy.pac (env: UNITY_PROXY) |
| `--proxy-disable` | — | False | False | — | Disable proxy for this invocation, ignoring all sources |
| `--log-proxy` | — | False | False | — | Log every outbound request to proxy-request.json for this run. Off by default; typically used once when reproducing a proxy issue for support. Also settable via UNITY_LOG_PROXY=1 or the proxyRequestLogging user setting. (env: UNITY_LOG_PROXY) |
| `--no-log-proxy` | — | False | False | — | Opt out of --log-proxy / UNITY_LOG_PROXY / the persisted user setting for this run. Use when logging is enabled globally but you want one clean invocation. |

### Environment variables

| Variable | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `UNITY_FORMAT` | Mirrors --format. |
| `UNITY_NO_BANNER` | Mirrors --no-banner. |
| `UNITY_NON_INTERACTIVE` | Mirrors --non-interactive. |
| `UNITY_QUIET` | Mirrors --quiet. |
| `UNITY_VERBOSE` | Mirrors --verbose. |
| `UNITY_PROXY` | Mirrors --proxy. |
| `UNITY_LOG_PROXY` | Mirrors --log-proxy. |

### Examples exposed by help

`unity mcp configure --list`
`unity mcp configure claude`
`unity mcp configure cursor --local`
`unity mcp configure claude --project-path /path/to/MyProject`
`unity mcp configure vscode --dry-run`

### Exit codes

None.

### Additional help sections

#### Supported clients

```text
  claude          Claude Desktop (global)
  claude-code     Claude Code CLI (via claude mcp add)
  cursor          Cursor — global (~/.cursor/mcp.json)
  cursor --local  Cursor — project-local (.cursor/mcp.json)
  vscode          VS Code / GitHub Copilot (.vscode/mcp.json)
  vscode-insiders VS Code Insiders / GitHub Copilot
  copilot-cli     GitHub Copilot CLI
  windsurf        Windsurf — global
  windsurf --local Windsurf — project-local
  cline           Cline VS Code extension
  codex           OpenAI Codex CLI
  kiro            AWS Kiro IDE
  trae            Trae (prints manual instructions)
  openclaw        OpenClaw (prints manual instructions)
  antigravity     Antigravity 2.0 / Antigravity IDE
  zed             Zed editor
  continue        Continue VS Code extension
  inspect         Launch MCP Inspector in browser

```


### Platform-specific differences

- **windows:** differs in arguments, extra_sections.

Confirm behavior with `unity mcp configure --help` on the target machine before execution.
